steps.title: Map the contrast in the sentence
steps.content:
The sentence sets up a comparison: vibration and warming are called "harmless applications," and they are contrasted with a stimulus that "seems less benign" (electric shock). The blank must describe the vibration/warming stimuli in a way that captures their harmless nature and makes the contrast land. Eliminate words that introduce unrelated ideas.
  • Choice A — deceptive: Means misleading or intended to deceive. Nothing in the passage suggests the harmless stimuli trick anyone; they simply offer less relief. ✗
  • Choice B — innocuous: Means harmless and inoffensive — directly synonymous with "harmless applications" already in the sentence. Perfectly captures the contrast with "less benign." ✓
  • Choice C — novel: Means new or original. The text is about effectiveness, not how recently vibration, warmth, or electricity were invented. ✗
  • Choice D — impractical: Means not suitable for use. The passage says these treatments do offer some temporary relief, so calling them impractical contradicts the text. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the word fits the logic
steps.content: The passage's paradox is that a harsher stimulus provides more relief than mild, harmless ones. The blank needs the word that best echoes "harmless" and sharpens that contrast with "less benign." That confirms B is correct.
